data.title: Investors eye Bank of America's Q4 earnings expecting 96 cents per share, $27.62B revenue. Annual dividend yield is 1.99%, $500 monthly possible.
data.description: Investors are closely watching Bank of America (NYSE: BAC) as they prepare for its upcoming earnings report. Analysts predict the bank will announce fourth-quarter earnings of 96 cents per share when they release their report on January 14. This represents an increase from the 82 cents per share reported in the same period last year. The consensus estimate for Bank of America's quarterly revenue stands at $27.62 billion, up from the $25.35 billion recorded last year. Currently, Bank of America offers an annual dividend yield of 1.99%, equating to a quarterly dividend of 28 cents per share ($1.12 annually). Investors are exploring ways to leverage this dividend yield to earn a consistent $500 per month. For more details, readers can find the complete story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: UK subsidiary of Ripple is approved as Electronic Money Institution, allowing it to offer payment services, but some crypto activities have restrictions.
data.description: The UK subsidiary of Ripple has obtained regulatory approval as an Electronic Money Institution to offer payment services. However, it is subject to restrictions on specific cryptocurrency operations. The approval allows Ripple's UK arm to facilitate financial transactions and provide payment solutions within established guidelines. This development marks a significant milestone for Ripple in the UK market, as it enhances the company's ability to engage in electronic money activities. While this approval opens up avenues for expanded payment services, the subsidiary must adhere to limitations on certain crypto-related functions to ensure compliance with regulatory frameworks.

data.title: Research report on Aircraft Safety MRO projects market to reach $701.9 million by 2035, growing at 3.5% CAGR.
data.description: In Dublin, a report on the Aircraft Safety MRO Market has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The market was valued at US$503.3 million in 2024 and is projected to reach US$701.9 million by 2035, with a steady CAGR of 3.5% anticipated during the forecast period. Factors driving this growth include the advancement of digital technologies, the expansion of global fleet sizes, and an increase in demand for aircraft safety maintenance, repair, and operations services.

data.title: EpicQuest Education honored with CES Speed Award for global branding by pioneering AI integration in higher education.
data.description: EpicQuest Education Group International Limited (Nasdaq: EEIQ) has been honored for its successful internationalization strategy, which includes exporting academic programs and leading initiatives that incorporate artificial intelligence into higher education. The award underscores the company's efforts in expanding globally and innovating in the education sector. This recognition comes as of January 9, 2026, in Toledo, Ohio. EpicQuest has demonstrated its commitment to advancements in education, particularly in AI integration, setting a precedent in the industry.

data.title: EU reaches groundbreaking free-trade deal with 4 South American countries: Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay. Significant move for global cooperation.
data.description: A pact involving Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay indicates a joint effort for enhanced international collaboration, showcasing a departure from the approach of the United States, which has been resorting to pressure tactics in its interactions with other nations.

data.title: Brado and Santé Ventures partner to create Brado AI, a tech entity using AI to extend provider reach in healthcare.
data.description: In response to a strategic investment partnership, Brado, a healthcare technology company, has launched Brado AI, a new dedicated technology entity focused on expanding provider reach through conversational AI. The partnership, announced on January 9, 2026, aims to enhance healthcare engagement.

data.title: Lone Star Fund buys Coral Gables office complex in Florida. Totaling 326,451-square-feet, the Class A property is in downtown Coral Gables, FL.
data.description: Lone Star Funds has finalized the purchase of The Alhambra, an office complex in South Florida, in collaboration with Highline Real Estate Capital LLC and Square2 Capital LLC. The acquisition was completed by an affiliate of Lone Star Real Estate Fund VII, L.P. The Class A property, situated in downtown Coral Gables, FL, within the Miami-Dade area, consists of two buildings adjacent to a Hyatt Regency Hotel, with a combined area of 326,451 square feet.

data.title: Franklin Street Prop declares $0.01 quarterly dividend for period Oct 1, 2025-Dec 31, 2025. Payable Feb 12, 2026 to stockholders.
data.description: In Wakefield, Massachusetts, Franklin Street Properties Corp. (NYSE American: FSP) revealed that its Board of Directors has approved a quarterly dividend of $0.01 per common stock share for the quarter spanning from October 1, 2025, to December 31, 2025. The dividend will be distributed on February 12, 2026, to shareholders registered as of January 23, 2026. Additional updates regarding FSP can be accessed on their website, www.fspreit.com. Information related to the company is frequently shared on the site.

data.title: Bain Capital and BlueWater Marinas acquire Kent Narrows Boatel, the fourth marina in their portfolio.
data.description: BlueWater Marinas, based in Boston and Charleston, has recently acquired Kent Narrows Boatel (KNB), a top-notch heated drystack marina near Annapolis, Maryland. KNB, a Class-A facility opened in 2017, has quickly established itself as a vital part of the local boating scene. This marks the fourth marina to join the Bain Capital/BlueWater group. The marina is strategically located outside Annapolis and boasts high-quality operations and excellent facilities. Located near the Bay Bridge, KNB further enhances the portfolio of BlueWater Marinas.
